step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the provided rationale; however, let me add some clarity regarding why we need more frequent stimuli beyond just avoiding overlap. At higher frequencies like those suggested here (>2Hz), muscles can better maintain their force output because they have less recovery time between activations before reaching another peak contractile state. This continuous activation helps sustain stronger overall tension compared to lower frequencies which may lead to fatigue sooner due to incomplete rest periods between stimulations. Given these points about improved efficiency and sustainability at faster rates, my agreement stands firm on selecting Option B as the best fit among given choices for achieving efficient tetany while minimizing risk of overlapped signals within each muscle fiber’s operational cycle.
